$comment: D-CSUBSET-VLA C4a-param: a PARAMETER pointer-to-VLA `int (*p)[n]` (and the adjusted `int a[][n]`) RUNS on all 3 legs. The callee body's subscript p[i][j] scales by the RUNTIME pointee row size (n*sizeof(int)), frozen ONCE at the callee prologue from the incoming `n` param (the C3/C4a-local vlaStrideSlot + scaleIndexToBytes substrate, now populated for a ptr-to-VLA PARAM). A ptr-to-VLA param emits only a FIXED 8-byte stride slot (no dynamic-stack object), so g and h are NOT leaf-restricted — each CALLS `sink` freely (the C1b non-leaf-VLA gate keys on `sub_sp_reg`, which a ptr param never emits). The storage is a FIXED buffer cast to a ptr-to-VLA row so main holds NO VLA OBJECT and may CALL: a genuine VLA object `int b[K][n]` DOES decay to `int (*)[n]` and type-checks (pinned semantically, zero S_TypeMismatch), but a caller that holds a VLA object AND makes a call is a NON-leaf VLA function, which the C1b frame model does not yet build (D-CSUBSET-VLA-NONLEAF-CALL-FRAME) — casting isolates THIS feature from that orthogonal deferral. The KEY correctness witness is the OFF-DIAGONAL p[1][0] vs p[0][2] (DISTINCT cells) — a wrong row stride would alias/transpose them — cross-verified through buf's raw cells after the call. `volatile vn` defeats const-fold so n is genuinely runtime. The arm64 leg proves the runtime-stride math on the non-host target (agnostic MIR); the `release` arm proves the stride Loads survive the full optimizer (never folded to a constant). exitCode 42; return 1..14 are strict in-program pins.
